safeguards.comparative: Headline result is Gaussian vs a fat-cosine baseline on THIS same set (Stage B). Any bias in these labels applies equally to both systems, so the delta is trustworthy even if absolute numbers are soft.
safeguards.ground_truth: Relevance is defined by the specific stored memory (match_texts / gold_ids), NOT by what retrieval returns. Domain labels are ignored for correctness — the store's domain taxonomy is known-noisy.
safeguards.frozen: Authored from memory_list dumps on 2026-07-08 BEFORE running any retrieval scoring. Do not edit entries to improve a score; add a v2 file instead.
safeguards.published: Committed in-repo so labels can be inspected and challenged.

matching: Stage A matches by normalized text-containment (lowercase, alnum+space). Any returned row whose text contains a gold match_text counts as a hit for that match_text. This credits near-duplicate memories automatically. gold_ids are recorded for Stage B ID-level matching + traceability.

categories.exact: distinct fact stated plainly in one memory
categories.paraphrase: query wording differs from the memory wording — tests semantics over keywords
categories.multihop: several distinct related memories should surface together — tests spreading activation
categories.temporal: a fact that was later revised — the CURRENT/newer memory should win
categories.adversarial: no relevant memory exists — the right behavior is low-confidence / abstain
